speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Glamis thou art, and Cawdor, and shalt be

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: What thou art promised. Yet do I fear thy nature;

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: It is too full o’ th’ milk of human kindness

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: To catch the nearest way. Thou wouldst be great,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Art not without ambition, but without

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: The illness should attend it. What thou wouldst highly,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That wouldst thou holily; wouldst not play false

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And yet wouldst wrongly win. Thou ’dst have, great Glamis,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That which cries “Thus thou must do,” if thou have it,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And that which rather thou dost fear to do,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Than wishest should be undone. Hie thee hither,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That I may pour my spirits in thine ear

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And chastise with the valor of my tongue

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: All that impedes thee from the golden round,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Which fate and metaphysical aid doth seem

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: To have thee crowned withal.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: What is your tidings?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The King comes here tonight.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Thou ’rt mad to say it.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Is not thy master with him, who, were ’t so,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Would have informed for preparation?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: So please you, it is true. Our thane is coming.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: One of my fellows had the speed of him,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Who, almost dead for breath, had scarcely more

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Than would make up his message.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Give him tending.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: He brings great news.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: The raven himself is hoarse

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That croaks the fatal entrance of Duncan

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Under my battlements. Come, you spirits

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That tend on mortal thoughts, unsex me here,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And fill me from the crown to the toe top-full

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Of direst cruelty. Make thick my blood.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Stop up th’ access and passage to remorse,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That no compunctious visitings of nature

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Shake my fell purpose, nor keep peace between

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Th’ effect and it. Come to my woman’s breasts

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And take my milk for gall, you murd’ring ministers,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Wherever in your sightless substances

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: You wait on nature’s mischief. Come, thick night,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And pall thee in the dunnest smoke of hell,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: That my keen knife see not the wound it makes,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Nor heaven peep through the blanket of the dark

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: To cry “Hold, hold!”

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Great Glamis, worthy Cawdor,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Greater than both by the all-hail hereafter!

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Thy letters have transported me beyond

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: This ignorant present, and I feel now

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: The future in the instant.

speaker: MACBETH
text: My dearest love,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Duncan comes here tonight.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And when goes hence?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Tomorrow, as he purposes.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: O, never

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Shall sun that morrow see!

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Your face, my thane, is as a book where men

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: May read strange matters. To beguile the time,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Look like the time. Bear welcome in your eye,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Your hand, your tongue. Look like th’ innocent flower,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: But be the serpent under ’t. He that’s coming

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Must be provided for; and you shall put

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: This night’s great business into my dispatch,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Which shall to all our nights and days to come

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Give solely sovereign sway and masterdom.

speaker: MACBETH
text: We will speak further.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Only look up clear.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: To alter favor ever is to fear.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Leave all the rest to me.
